knownIssues.title: Pimax Play / PiTool software can be unstable
knownIssues.description: Pimax's companion software has a history of stability issues, driver conflicts, and bugs introduced by updates. SteamVR may have display issues with certain Pimax software versions.
knownIssues.workaround: Check Pimax forums for stable software version recommendations before updating. Keep Pimax Play and SteamVR versions in sync. Some users keep a known-stable version archived.

knownIssues.title: 120Hz requires Display Stream Compression — not all GPUs or cables support it
knownIssues.description: Running at 120Hz with 2880x2880 per eye exceeds raw DP 1.4 bandwidth and requires DSC. Some older GPUs or cables may not reliably support DSC, causing flickering or connection drops.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a certified DP 1.4 cable (not 1.2 or passive adapters). Verify DSC support in Pimax Play. Run at 90Hz if 120Hz produces instability.

knownIssues.title: Inside-out tracking has limited range and accuracy vs. lighthouse
knownIssues.description: The Crystal Light's built-in inside-out tracking does not match the precision of SteamVR Lighthouse tracking. Fast motion or complex environments may cause controller drift.
knownIssues.workaround: Add SteamVR Lighthouse base stations and use a VIVE tracker for superior tracking accuracy. The Crystal Light supports Lighthouse via optional face-plate accessory or native on Crystal (non-Light) variant.

optimizationTips.tip: Use PimaxXR runtime for best performance in OpenXR titles
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Pimax's own VR software is optimized for Crystal Light and gives better performance than routing everything through SteamVR.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: PimaxXR is a native OpenXR runtime that bypasses SteamVR overhead for OpenXR games. Set PimaxXR as the active OpenXR runtime in Pimax Play. This reduces compositor overhead by 2-4ms frame time versus the SteamVR runtime on compatible titles.

optimizationTips.tip: Set SteamVR render resolution to 100% — don't supersample
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: At this high resolution, turning up the picture quality setting above normal won't make things look much better and will significantly hurt performance.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: 2880x2880 per eye is already near or at the resolving limit of the optics. Supersampling above 100% in SteamVR provides negligible visual gain while causing large increases in render time (rendering 2880x2880 is already ~40% more pixels than 2560x2560). Reserve GPU budget for higher framerates.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable Smart Smoothing in Pimax Play as your reprojection safety net
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Smart Smoothing in Pimax's software will insert smoothed frames when your PC struggles to keep up, keeping VR comfortable.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Pimax Smart Smoothing works similarly to SteamVR motion smoothing — it synthesizes intermediate frames when the render thread misses the display deadline. Enable in Pimax Play per-application. More effective at 90Hz than 120Hz due to the larger time budget for frame synthesis.
